title: iria uliginosa
intro: Iria uliginosa, also known as marsh iria or bog iris, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Iria uliginosa include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and wound healer. In herbal preparations, Iria uliginosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
